No other Appearance.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling as Hearing required.

Hearing is held. Counsel informs the court of timeline; Before the settlement was finalized a conflict arose; there is an executed conditional settlement. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Maxwell Law, KC Maxwell, Ira Bibbero's Moton to be Relieved as counsel for PLanitiffs John Deakins and Melanie Deakins.

Granted. The court signs two orders.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Action
Off Calendar Jun-22-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS YOURPEOPLE, INC. AND PAYYOURPEOPLE LLC's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Action.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to compel arbitration is denied. Defendant's motion to stay is granted.

"[A] nonsignatory defendant may invoke an arbitration clause to compel a signatory plaintiff to arbitrate its claims when the causes of action against the nonsignatory are 'intimately founded in and intertwined' with the underlying contract obligations. [citations] By relying on contract terms in a claim against a nonsignatory defendant, even if not exclusively, a plaintiff may be equitably estopped from repudiating the arbitration clause contained in that agreement." (Boucher v. All. Title Co. (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 262, 271-272.) A nonsignatory's general reference to an agreement with an arbitration clause in its complaint is insufficient by itself to compel the nonsignatory to arbitrate based on equitable estoppel. (See Goldman v. KPMG, LLP (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 209, 218.) Something more than but for causation is needed. (See DMS Service, LLC v. Superior Court (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 1346, 1356-1357.)

Here, plaintiff's complaint does not rely on the Dynamic agreement nor is it intimately founded in and intertwined with that agreement. The gravamen of plaintiff's action is based on defendant's failure to remedy the alleged IRS problem it created. Plaintiff's claims are sufficiently separate from the Dynamic contract such that equitable estoppel does not apply.

Defendant's motion to stay pursuant to CCP 1281.4 is denied. Under CCP 1281.4, the court may issue a stay where a court "has ordered arbitration." The statute is inapplicable since no court has ordered arbitration.

Nevertheless, the court concludes that there is a sufficient overlap between this action and the pending arbitration between Dynamic and defendants to warrant a stay based on the court's "implicit power." (See Seidman & Seidman v. Wolfson (1975) 50 Cal.App.3d 826, 833-34.) A stay will conserve party and court resources and prevent the possibility of conflicting rulings.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
